data.narrative:
Neros, a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Soren Monroe-Anderson and Olaf Hichwa, has raised $121M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all operating at the Series B stage within Defense & Security.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a baseline proxy, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $230M. Peer-median analysis anchors to a cohort of 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.2B and a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. This method reflects market pricing for established players in the defense-tech space.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $195M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $715M. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between the capital-raised methodology and peer multiples. Key uncertainties include whether Neros tracks closer to earlier-stage or established peer valuations, the strength of its revenue trajectory relative to the five named comparables, and sector-specific risk factors in defense procurement. Additional due diligence on revenue, growth rates, and competitive positioning would be required to narrow this range.
